thread.title: New Cape Girardeau Police Chief looking forward to getting the ball rolling
thread.title_full: New Cape Girardeau Police Chief looking forward to getting the ball rolling

author: @kfvsnews
title: New Cape Girardeau Police Chief looking forward to getting the ball rolling
text: CAPE GIRARDEAU, Mo. (KFVS) - Cape Girardeau city leaders announce a familiar face will serve as the new Police Chief. Since August, Adam Glueck served as the Interim Police Chief for the Cape Girardeau Police Department. Now that position is permanent and he’s the new leader in charge. Chief Glueck said he did not expect this announcement today , but he’s ready to get the ball rolling in his new role. “I’m ready to fill that role. I understand it’s a huge responsibility,” said Chief Adam Glueck. Glueck is looking to pick up where former Police Chief Wes Blair left off. “He had a lot of relationships built up with the different community members and different organizations,” Glueck said. “So going around trying to continue those relationships and build on them.” City Manager, Dr. Kenny Haskin said Glueck’s 25-year history with the department speaks to his readiness for the position. “When you think about Adam, you think about a blue-collar guy, a person who worked his way up through the ranks,” Dr. Haskin said. Haskin said the new chief’s understanding of community-oriented policing will help the city as well. “It’s a concept that’s worked from what I see and what Adam brings to the table is a mindset that truly gets it,” Haskin said. “He understands how important and how valuable that connection is with our community.” Chief Glueck said putting resources into what he calls “hot spot” areas is leading his priority list. “I think Cape Girardeau was a relatively safe town, but we do have some areas where some areas are more prone to issues than others, and those are the areas that I want to concentrate our resources on,” Glueck said. Glueck’s goal is to have a safer community for everyone who lives in Cape Girardeau. “We’re going to do everything we can that’s within our power to make sure that people feel safer and welcome in our community,” Glueck said. Glueck’s role begins effective immediately and he says there’s already plans on making changes to better suit the department. Copyright 2024 KFVS. All rights reserved.
